whenToUse: Chat scroll area when matching page

brandcolor-surface.usage: Raised cards, sticky footers, header strips on content.

whenToUse: Reserved for modals / high-contrast chips if added later

brandcolor-marrow.usage: Deep Marrow — brand ink (not used for top nav chrome; see nav-chrome).
whenToUse: Strong text on warm backgrounds when you need explicit marrow

brandcolor-nav-chrome.usage: App header background — constant in light and dark OS preference.
brandcolor-nav-chrome.importantNote: Keeps Deep Marrow bar even when dark theme flips other tokens.

brandcolor-nav-chrome-fg.usage: Text and controls on the marrow nav bar.

brandcolor-primary.usage: Sear — primary CTA, active nav link, sliders, send buttons.
whenToUse: Accent on Phosphor icons in nav
examples: bg-brandcolor-primary text-brandcolor-primary-foreground hover:bg-brandcolor-primary-hover rounded-full px-5 py-2

brandcolor-primary-hover.usage: Darker Sear for hover on primary buttons.
brandcolor-primary-hover.logic: Pair every bg-brandcolor-primary interactive control with hover:bg-brandcolor-primary-hover.

brandcolor-primary-foreground.usage: Text on Sear buttons and user chat bubbles.

brandcolor-bone.usage: Bone — soft fills, EOD panel, totals panels, code chips on errors.
examples: Assistant bubble uses brandcolor-chat-assistant (same family in light mode)

brandcolor-tallow.usage: Tallow — hover on nav links, secondary emphasis.
whenToUse: Nav link hover on marrow bar

brandcolor-text-strong.hex: Contextual (marrow in light, salt in dark)

brandcolor-text-weak.usage: Secondary descriptions, captions, table header labels.

brandcolor-stroke-strong.usage: Strong borders (inputs if you need more weight later).
whenToUse: Optional emphasis borders — app mostly uses strokeweak

brandcolor-strokeweak.importantNote: Prefer this over gray Tailwind palette for all borders.

brandcolor-divider.usage: Reserved for section dividers if needed.

brandcolor-neutralhover.usage: Hover on neutral filled buttons (e.g. EOD secondary button).

brandcolor-banner-warning-bg.usage: Warm warning strip backgrounds (no cool blue).

primary.completeClasses: rounded-full bg-brandcolor-primary px-4 py-2 text-sm font-semibold text-brandcolor-primary-foreground hover:bg-brandcolor-primary-hover disabled:opacity-60
primary.whenToUse: Main submit actions (Send, Save check-in, Apply, Try again).

ghostOnNav.completeClasses: rounded-full border border-white/25 px-3 py-1.5 text-sm text-brandcolor-nav-chrome-fg hover:bg-white/10

card.whenToUse: History rows, report filters, meal cards.

input.classes: rounded-xl border border-brandcolor-strokeweak bg-brandcolor-fill px-3 py-2 text-sm text-brandcolor-text-strong

workflow: STEP 1: Read src/app/globals.css — :root and @theme inline for --color-brandcolor-* tokens (light palette only; no system dark theme swap).
STEP 2: Read themeguide.json themeColors and componentGuidelines.
STEP 3: Implement UI using only bg-brandcolor-*, text-brandcolor-*, border-brandcolor-*, font-serif, font-sans.
STEP 4: Verify no hex literals, no Tailwind default palette (gray/zinc/teal/emerald/sky), no inline style colors.

noHexInComponents.rule: Do not paste hex colors into JSX class strings; use theme tokens.

noCoolDefaultPalette.rule: Do not use bg-gray-*, text-zinc-*, bg-teal-*, etc.

noTailwindConfigJs.rule: Theme extension is only in globals.css for this repo.
